examples: Unless smokers are consistently located near more hospitable warm-water vents, chemosynthesis can account for only a fraction of the vent faunas.
This suggests that bacterial chemosynthesis is not a sufficient source of nutrition for these creatures.
The bacteria, well the bacteria kind of feed the tubeworms through chemosynthesis, remember, that chemical reaction I described earlier.
Where there is no light, as in the deep sea, creatures rely on chemical energy (or chemosynthesis).
